framework_constants.H0_active_branch: Paper 10 legacy projected-observer baseline used by Paper 34 v1.0/v1.1

published_h0_measurements.notes: Paper 34 uses the rounded base-LambdaCDM comparison H0 = 67.4 +/- 0.5.

published_h0_measurements.assignment_basis: geometric luminosity-distance method with no stellar photometric calibrator leg
published_h0_measurements.source: Improved constraint on the Hubble constant from dark sirens with LIGO/Virgo/KAGRA O4a
published_h0_measurements.notes: The public bundle uses the +4.1 uncertainty as the symmetric pull denominator.

published_h0_measurements.assignment_basis: direct stellar-photometric standard-candle chain with one uncancelled photometric leg
published_h0_measurements.source: Status Report on the Chicago-Carnegie Hubble Program (CCHP): Measurement of the Hubble Constant Using the Hubble and James Webb Space Telescopes

published_h0_measurements.assignment_basis: non-geometric Fermat/Weyl class at alpha=2 with centered lens normalization
published_h0_measurements.source: TDCOSMO 2025: Cosmological constraints from strong lensing time delays
published_h0_measurements.notes: The paper reports H0 = 71.6 +3.9/-3.3; this bundle uses the average 3.6 as the symmetric pull denominator.

published_h0_measurements.assignment_basis: Cepheid/SN ladder with two uncancelled photometric calibrator legs
published_h0_measurements.source: SH0ES / Pantheon+ local distance ladder comparison used by Paper 34
published_h0_measurements.notes: Paper 34 uses the rounded comparison H0 = 73.0 +/- 1.0.

published_h0_measurements.assignment_basis: TRGB-calibrated SN ladder with two uncancelled photometric calibrator legs
published_h0_measurements.source: The Perfect Host: JWST Cepheid Observations in a Background-free Type Ia Supernova Host Confirm No Bias in Hubble-constant Measurements
published_h0_measurements.notes: The abstract reports the combined HST/JWST Cepheid plus TRGB-calibrated SN value H0 = 73.18 +/- 0.88.
